_description: Compact Splatoon 3 game world context for LLM translation prompt injection. Provides domain knowledge to help the LLM make contextually accurate translations.
game_overview: Splatoon 3 is a team-based ink shooter by Nintendo set 12,000 years after human extinction. Players are Inklings (squid-kids) or Octolings (octopus-kids) who battle by spreading colored ink. The game's tone is youthful, energetic, and fashion-forward — 'freshness' is the core cultural value.

key_concepts.inking: Players spread colored ink on surfaces. Standing in your team's ink lets you swim through it quickly (squid form). Enemy ink slows you and deals damage.
key_concepts.splatting: The Splatoon term for 'killing' — players get 'splatted', not killed. They respawn after a few seconds.
key_concepts.squid_form: Players can transform between kid form (humanoid, can shoot) and squid/octopus form (can swim in ink, faster movement, refill ink tank).
key_concepts.special_moves: Squid Roll (dodge with invincibility), Squid Surge (climb walls fast), Super Jump (teleport to teammates).

game_modes.turf_war: Casual 4v4 mode. Team that inks the most ground in 3 minutes wins.
game_modes.anarchy_battle: Competitive ranked mode with 4 sub-modes: Splat Zones (control area), Tower Control (ride the tower), Rainmaker (carry the weapon to enemy base), Clam Blitz (throw clams into enemy basket).
game_modes.x_battle: Elite ranked mode above Anarchy. Uses X Power rating.
game_modes.salmon_run: 4-player PvE co-op mode. Fight waves of Salmonid enemies, collect Golden Eggs, meet quota. Has Boss Salmonids and King Salmonids (Cohozuna, Horrorboros, Megalodontia).
game_modes.splatfest: Periodic 3-team event where players vote on a theme and battle for their team. Hosted by Deep Cut (the idol group).

characters.deep_cut: Splatoon 3's idol group (すりみ連合). Shiver (フウカ) — cool shark tamer, Frye (ウツホ) — energetic eel dancer, Big Man (マンタロー) — gentle manta ray who only says 'Ay!' (エイ!).
characters.squid_sisters: Splatoon 1 idols (シオカラーズ). Callie (アオリ) and Marie (ホタル) — still appear in story mode.
characters.off_the_hook: Splatoon 2 idols (テンタクルズ). Pearl (ヒメ) — loud and tiny, Marina (イイダ) — shy DJ octopus. Key roles in Side Order DLC.
characters.mr_grizz: Bear who runs Grizzco Industries (Salmon Run). Turns out to be the main villain of the story mode.
characters.sheldon: Weapon shop owner (ブキチ). Extremely enthusiastic about weapons.

translation_tone_guide.general: Splatoon commentary is fast, excited, and casual. Speakers often shout, use abbreviations, and mix gaming jargon with everyday Japanese. Translations should feel natural and energetic — not stiff or formal.
translation_tone_guide.register: Most JP Splatoon YouTubers use casual speech (タメ口) with occasional polite forms. Match this with casual English/Chinese. Use contractions, short sentences, and gaming vocabulary.
translation_tone_guide.exclamations: Preserve the energy of exclamations. ナイス! = Nice! / 来た! = Let's go! / やばい = Oh no / That's insane (context-dependent). やった = Got 'em!

ambiguous_terms.エリア: Usually means 'Splat Zones' (the game mode), NOT a generic area. In rare cases when discussing map positioning it can mean 'area/zone'.
ambiguous_terms.アサリ: Usually means 'Clam Blitz' (the game mode), NOT literal clams.
ambiguous_terms.ホコ: Always means 'Rainmaker' in Splatoon context, NOT a generic torch/halberd.
ambiguous_terms.バイト: In Salmon Run context means 'shift/job' (playing Salmon Run), NOT a part-time job.
ambiguous_terms.キャンプ: In weapon context means 'Tenta Brella', NOT camping/tent. In strategy context means 'camping (staying in one spot)'.
ambiguous_terms.スパイ: In weapon context means 'Undercover Brella', NOT spy.
ambiguous_terms.スシ: Always means 'Splattershot' in Splatoon context, NEVER sushi.
ambiguous_terms.わかば: Always means 'Splattershot Jr.' in Splatoon context, NOT young leaves.
ambiguous_terms.ジム: In weapon context means 'Splatana Stamper', NOT a gym.
ambiguous_terms.ドライブ: In weapon context means 'Splatana Wiper', NOT driving.
ambiguous_terms.環境: In competitive context means 'the meta' (current strongest strategies/weapons), NOT the environment.

commentary_patterns.video_structure: JP Splatoon videos typically: greeting → topic intro → gameplay with commentary → conclusion. Common phrases: はいどうも (hey everyone), というわけで (so with that said), 解説 (guide/breakdown), 攻略 (strategy tips).
commentary_patterns.damage_notation: 確N or N確 = N-shot kill. Example: 確1 = one-shot kill, 3確 = three-shot kill. 疑似確 = pseudo one-shot (using damage-up abilities to reduce shots needed).
commentary_patterns.rank_references: Players often reference ranks: S+, X Battle, XP (X Power) with numbers like XP2500. ウデマエ = rank. 盛る = climbing/gaining points.
commentary_patterns.salmon_run_terms: バイト = shift, ノルマ = quota, 納品 = deliver eggs, 寄せ = lure bosses to basket, 野良 = random teammates, 干潮/満潮 = low/high tide.

jp_en_divergences: スピナー (Spinner) = Splatling in English
シェルター (Shelter) = Brella in English
ワイパー (Wiper) = Splatana in English
マニューバー (Maneuver) = Dualies in English
